endpoints.description: YouTube search result page. Runtime compacts ytInitialData into video cards with title, channel, views, published time, duration, videoId, URL, and available filter links. For multi-topic or multi-filter tasks, prefer collect_youtube_search_results when available so the runtime can batch search and save JSON files.
strategy_notes: Open youtube.com once, then use collect_youtube_search_results for tasks that ask for many topics/categories or saved JSON files.
For single-query tasks, compact a /results URL into videoRenderer cards and filter links without relying on the heavy accessibility tree.
Do not open video pages or download media for search-result collection tasks.

runtime_strategies.name: youtube_batch_search_results
runtime_strategies.trigger: YouTube search tasks with multiple topics, categories, upload-date filters, duration filters, sort orders, or saved JSON outputs.

actions.description: Call collect_youtube_search_results once with all requested searches. Set query, limit, sort, upload_date, duration, and file_name for each search.
For single search pages, compact https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=<encoded query> into video cards and filter links.

observable_validation: Saved JSON files exist for every requested topic/category when the task asks to save files.
Each compact result row includes a YouTube watch URL or videoId plus title.
The final answer should be based on the savedFiles list and compact rows, not repeated YouTube scrolling.
notes: The runtime only reads search result pages; it does not download or automate video playback.
